params.name: sessionId
params.description: An optional browser session identifier to reference an existing running browser sessions. When passed, the tool will be executed on the provided browser session.

items.docs: Start from a URL and perform the given task.

params.name: workflowId
params.description: The workflow ID returned when starting an asynchronous perform-web-task execution.

items.docs: Get the status of an asynchronous perform-web-task execution by workflow ID.

params.name: page
params.description: Page number to fetch.

params.name: limit
params.description: Number of sessions per page. Supported values are 10, 20, and 50.

params.name: sort_by
params.description:
Sort field. Common values are `created_at`, `session_id`, `used_credits`,
`created_at`, and `api_key_name`.

params.name: sort_order
params.description: Sort direction.

params.name: search
params.description: Comma-separated tag search terms (partial, case-insensitive).

params.name: status
params.description: Exact session status filter.

params.name: tags
params.description: Comma-separated tag list. Session must include all provided tags.

params.name: domains
params.description: Comma-separated domain list. Session must include all provided domains.

params.name: created_from
params.description: Include sessions created at or after this timestamp (ISO 8601).

params.name: created_to
params.description: Include sessions created at or before this timestamp (ISO 8601).

params.name: batch_id
params.description: Filter by batch identifier.

params.name: task_initiated
params.description: Filter by whether the session was task-initiated.

params.name: playground
params.description: Filter by whether the session is a playground session.

params.name: proxy
params.description: Filter by whether proxy was active for the session.

params.name: extra_stealth
params.description: Filter by whether extra stealth mode was active.

params.name: profile_name
params.description: Case-insensitive partial match on browser profile name.

items.docs:
Retrieves a paginated list of sessions for the authenticated team with support for
sorting and advanced filtering. This endpoint mirrors the backend session history-page
filtering behavior.
Allocates a new browser session for the user, with optional configurations for ad-blocking, captcha solving, proxy usage, and idle timeout.

Non-blocking variant of `POST /v1/sessions`. Returns a `request_id`
immediately and provisions the underlying browser pod in the
background. Poll `GET /v1/sessions/async/{request_id}/status` until
`status` becomes `ready` to obtain the resolved `session_id`,
`cdp_url` and `live_view_url`.

params.name: request_id
params.description: The `request_id` returned from `POST /v1/sessions/async`.

items.docs:
Polling endpoint paired with `POST /v1/sessions/async`. Returns the
current lifecycle `status` of the async request and, once the pod is
up, the embedded `session` object containing `session_id`, `cdp_url`
and `live_view_url`.
Terminates all active browser sessions associated with the provided API key.

params.name: tags
params.description: Comma-separated list of session tags to filter by. Sessions must contain all provided tags.

params.name: domains
params.description: Comma-separated list of domains to filter by. Sessions must contain all provided domains.

params.name: created_from
params.description: Include sessions created at or after this timestamp (ISO 8601).

params.name: created_to
params.description: Include sessions created at or before this timestamp (ISO 8601).

params.name: batch_id
params.description: Filter sessions by batch identifier.

params.name: task_initiated
params.description: Filter by whether the session was initiated by a task.

params.name: playground
params.description: Filter by whether the session is a playground session.

params.name: proxy
params.description: Filter by whether proxy was active for the session.

params.name: extra_stealth
params.description: Filter by whether extra stealth mode was active for the session.

params.name: profile_name
params.description: Filter sessions by browser profile name (case-insensitive partial match).

items.docs: Retrieves status information for all browser sessions associated with the API key.

params.name: from_date
params.description: Start date for filtering (ISO 8601 format).

params.name: to_date
params.description: End date for filtering (ISO 8601 format).

params.name: granularity
params.description: Time granularity for aggregation.

params.name: metrics
params.description: Metrics to include in the response.

params.name: page
params.description: Page number for pagination.

params.name: limit
params.description: Number of records per page.

items.docs: Retrieves session usage analytics for the authenticated team.

items.docs: Retrieves detailed information about a specific browser session.

params.name: session_id
params.description: The ID of the browser session to end.

items.docs: Deletes the browser session associated with the provided browser session ID. Requires a valid API key for authentication.

params.name: session_id
params.description: The ID of the session to retrieve pages for.

items.docs: Retrieves a list of pages associated with a specific browser session.

params.name: sessionId
params.description: The browser session ID

items.docs:
Upload files directly to a browser session for use with web forms and file inputs.

Files are saved to the session's uploads directory and can be referenced in CDP commands.

params.name: session_id
params.description: The unique identifier of the browser session to retrieve downloads for.

items.docs: Retrieves metadata of files downloaded during a browser session. Requires a valid API key for authentication.

params.name: taskId
params.description: The ID of the task to run

items.docs: Triggers execution of a task by ID using the non-deprecated Tasks endpoints.

params.name: runId
params.description: The ID of the task run

items.docs: Retrieves the current status and result of a task run in the non-deprecated Tasks endpoints.

items.docs: Retrieves all identities associated with a specific application.

items.docs:
Creates an identity token for a specific application. This token is used to initiate
an authentication flow for linking user identities to the application.

The callback URL must use HTTPS and is where the user will be redirected after authentication.

params.name: validateAsync
params.description: Whether to validate the identity asynchronously. Defaults to true.

items.docs: Creates a new identity for the given source. Credentials are optional.

Fetches all stored profiles.
Creates a new profile from a browser session. A Profile stores cookies, local storage, and cache.

items.docs: Retrieves details of a specific profile by its name.

params.description: The name of the profile to delete.

params.name: sessionId
params.description: An optional browser session identifier to reference an existing running browser session. If provided, the tool will execute within that browser session.

items.docs: Retrieve the rendered content of a webpage in Markdown or HTML format. Text content is also extracted from PDFs.

Fetch fully-rendered page content from any URL — including bot-protected sites — without managing a browser session.
Requests are routed through stealth browsers with residential proxies, captcha solving, and fingerprint randomization.
No session is required. See the [Web Unlocker guide](/advanced/web-unlocker) for more details.

params.name: sessionId
params.description: An optional browser session identifier to reference an existing running browser sessions. When passed, the tool will be executed on the provided browser session.

items.docs: This endpoint captures a screenshot of the specified webpage using Chromium. Users can customize the viewport dimensions and capture options.

params.description: Optional browser session ID. If omitted, `url` in the request body is required.

items.docs: Generates a PDF of the current page for a browser session and returns it as a binary PDF file.

params.description: The ID of the browser session

items.docs: Takes a screenshot of the current browser session and returns it as an image.

params.name: sessionId
params.description: The browser session ID

items.docs:
List all resources that have been uploaded to the browser session for agent use.
Returns resource metadata including name, size, type, and last modified timestamp.

params.name: sessionId
params.description: The browser session ID

items.docs:
Upload files as agent resources to a browser session using multipart/form-data. 
If you upload a ZIP file, it will be automatically extracted and the files will be made available as agent resources.
If you upload a single file, it will be saved directly as an agent resource.
Resources are then accessible to AI agents for task completion and automation.

params.name: session_id
params.description: The ID of the browser session

items.docs: Pauses the AI agent for the specified browser session.

params.name: session_id
params.description: The ID of the browser session

items.docs: Resumes the AI agent for the specified browser session.
Get all extensions for the authenticated user
Upload a new browser extension as a ZIP file. The extension will be validated and stored for use in browser sessions.

params.name: id
params.description: The ID of the extension to retrieve

items.docs: Get details of a specific extension by its ID

params.name: id
params.description: The ID of the extension to delete

items.docs: Delete an extension and remove it from storage
Get all CA certificates uploaded for the team

Upload a CA certificate to trust in browser sessions. Certificates are managed at the team level.
Once uploaded, reference the certificate by name when creating sessions to trust services using that CA.

See [CA Certificates documentation](/advanced/ca-certificates) for more details.

params.name: name
params.description: The name of the certificate to delete

items.docs: Delete a CA certificate by name. The certificate will be removed from storage and can no longer be used in new sessions.
Retrieves all integrations for the authenticated team.

Creates a new integration with a third-party service like 1Password. 
The integration can then be used in browser sessions to automatically load secrets and credentials.

params.name: integrationId
params.description: The ID of the integration to delete

items.docs: Deletes an existing integration and removes its stored credentials.

params.name: event_name
params.description: The name of the event to wait for

items.docs:
Waits for a specific event to be signaled by another process, workflow, or session. 
This endpoint blocks until the event is signaled or the timeout is reached.
Useful for coordinating between multiple browser sessions or workflows.

params.name: event_name
params.description: The name of the event to signal

items.docs:
Signals an event with associated data, unblocking any clients waiting for this event.
This enables coordination between different browser sessions, workflows, or external processes.

params.name: status
params.description: Filter batches by status

params.name: page
params.description: Page number (1-based)

params.name: limit
params.description: Number of results per page

params.name: created_after
params.description: Filter batches created after this timestamp (ISO 8601)

params.name: created_before
params.description: Filter batches created before this timestamp (ISO 8601)

items.docs:
Lists all batch session requests for the authenticated team, with optional filtering
by status and pagination support.

Creates multiple browser sessions in a single batch operation. This endpoint allows you to
create up to 5,000 browser sessions simultaneously with the same configuration.

The batch will be processed asynchronously, and you can monitor progress using the batch status endpoint.

params.name: batch_id
params.description: The unique identifier of the batch

items.docs:
Retrieves detailed status information for a specific batch, including progress,
individual session details, and any errors that occurred.

params.name: batch_id
params.description: The unique identifier of the batch

items.docs:
Updates a batch session. Supports cancelling a running or pending batch,
which terminates all running sessions in the batch.

params.name: batch_id
params.description: The unique identifier of the batch

items.docs:
Deletes all sessions in a batch. Running sessions will be terminated
and all session data will be removed.

params.name: batch_id
params.description: The unique identifier of the batch

items.docs:
Retries failed sessions in a batch by creating new browser pods for each
failed session. The batch status will be set back to 'processing'.

params.name: page
params.description: Page number

params.name: limit
params.description: Number of tasks per page

items.docs:
Retrieves a paginated list of all tasks for the authenticated team. Tasks are returned 
with their latest version information and metadata.

If you are using the new Task Builder, see the non-deprecated Tasks endpoints in this API reference.

Creates a new task or updates an existing task with the same name. Tasks are reusable code snippets 
that can be executed in browser sessions. Tasks support versioning with draft and published versions.

If you are using the new Task Builder, see the non-deprecated Tasks endpoints in this API reference.

Executes a task in a browser session. The task can be run with a specific version or the latest version.
Optionally, you can provide an existing session ID or let the system create a new one.

If you are using the new Task Builder, see the non-deprecated Tasks endpoints in this API reference.

params.name: taskName
params.description: The name of the task to run

items.docs:
Executes a task by its name, always using the latest version. This is a convenience endpoint
for running tasks without needing to know the task ID.

If you are using the new Task Builder, see the non-deprecated Tasks endpoints in this API reference.

params.name: taskId
params.description: The ID of the task to retrieve

items.docs:
Retrieves task metadata without the code content. Useful for getting task information
without downloading the full task code.

If you are using the new Task Builder, see the non-deprecated Tasks endpoints in this API reference.

params.name: taskId
params.description: The ID of the task to update

items.docs:
Updates task metadata (name and description). This does not affect the task code or versions.

If you are using the new Task Builder, see the non-deprecated Tasks endpoints in this API reference.

params.name: taskId
params.description: The ID of the task to delete

items.docs:
Soft deletes a task and all its versions. The task will no longer be accessible
but the data is preserved for potential recovery.

If you are using the new Task Builder, see the non-deprecated Tasks endpoints in this API reference.

params.name: taskId
params.description: The ID of the task

items.docs:
Retrieves all versions of a specific task, including draft and published versions.

If you are using the new Task Builder, see the non-deprecated Tasks endpoints in this API reference.

params.name: taskId
params.description: The ID of the task

items.docs:
Retrieves the latest version of a task, including the full code content.

If you are using the new Task Builder, see the non-deprecated Tasks endpoints in this API reference.

params.name: taskId
params.description: The ID of the task

items.docs:
Retrieves the draft version of a task, including the full code content.

If you are using the new Task Builder, see the non-deprecated Tasks endpoints in this API reference.

params.name: taskId
params.description: The ID of the task

items.docs:
Creates or updates the draft version of a task. Draft versions are used for development
and testing before publishing.

If you are using the new Task Builder, see the non-deprecated Tasks endpoints in this API reference.

params.name: taskId
params.description: The ID of the task

items.docs:
Deploys a task by creating a new version with auto-incremented version number.
This is the recommended way to publish task changes.

If you are using the new Task Builder, see the non-deprecated Tasks endpoints in this API reference.

params.name: taskId
params.description: The ID of the task

params.name: page
params.description: Page number

params.name: limit
params.description: Number of results per page

params.name: status
params.description: Filter by execution status

params.name: version
params.description: Filter by task version

items.docs:
Retrieves execution history for a task, including success/failure status,
execution times, and outputs. Results can be filtered by version and status.

If you are using the new Task Builder, see the Tasks V2 endpoints in this API reference.

params.name: taskId
params.description: The ID of the task

params.name: executionId
params.description: The ID of the execution result

items.docs:
Retrieves a single execution result by its ID. This endpoint is useful for polling
execution status in async mode or retrieving detailed execution information.

If you are using the new Task Builder, see the Tasks V2 endpoints in this API reference.

params.name: taskId
params.description: The ID of the task

params.name: taskVersion
params.description: The version to retrieve (draft, latest, or version number)

items.docs:
Retrieves a specific version of a task, including the full code content.

If you are using the new Task Builder, see the Tasks V2 endpoints in this API reference.

params.name: taskId
params.description: The ID of the task

params.name: taskVersion
params.description: The version to publish (cannot be draft)

items.docs:
Publishes a specific version of a task. This creates a new version if it doesn't exist,
or updates an existing version's metadata.

If you are using the new Task Builder, see the non-deprecated Tasks endpoints in this API reference.

params.name: taskId
params.description: The ID of the task

params.name: taskVersion
params.description: The version to delete

items.docs:
Soft deletes a specific version of a task. The version will no longer be accessible
but the data is preserved for potential recovery.

If you are using the new Task Builder, see the Tasks V2 endpoints in this API reference.
Retrieves credit balance, current-period credit usage, tier, and billing limits for the authenticated project.
